clean_body: I'm joining forces with Democratic Members of the Judiciary Committee | Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. = Friend, In Congress, I am proud to serve on the Judiciary Committee — it’s a serious committee, engaged in serious work to protect the fundamental civil rights and freedoms of all Americans. Or at least it used to be. Now, though, the unfortunate reality is that the Judiciary Committee is being used by Chairman Jim Jordan for cheap political stunts and dangerous attacks on our U.S. Constitution. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = The difference could not be more clear: ❌ House Republicans like Jim Jordan are using their power to weaponize our Congress, obstruct independent Justice Department investigations, and waste taxpayer dollars launching baseless investigations for political clout and clickbait. ✅ House Democrats like Madeleine Dean, Jerry Nadler, Mary Gay Scanlon, Joe Neguse, Deborah Ross, Hank Johnson, and me are taking the opportunity to call for an end to the obstruction in the Judiciary Committee and get back on track to legislate on the most important issues facing Americans and their families. Jim Jordan and the House GOP are backed by deep-pocketed mega-donors and it is going to take everything in our power to defeat them in 2024 – we have grassroots supporters like you in our corner but we have to get to work now. = = My Democratic colleagues and I are not backing down. Instead, we are laser-focused on holding the line and on taking back the majority in the House. Thank you for being on our team, — Eric Swalwell | | | = = Text ERIC to 43033 to get text updates! Democrat Eric Swalwell proudly represents California's 14th Congressional District. He needs your support in order to keep protecting what's important: our healthcare, our paychecks, our future, and our democracy. | | = Eric runs a people-powered campaign that relies on grassroots supporters like you, and if he's going to win re-election, he's going to need your help. name: Greta Presley
subject: I wanted to share some pictures with you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brandon Presley Campaign
body:
I was so proud to see our ad shot on the little Main Street of Nettleton. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993849/418251499/17860314?refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)
---
Peter, I was so very proud to see Brandon's first TV ad go up earlier this month. It was shot on Main Street in downtown Nettleton, the little town that helped raise him and where he knows every crack in every sidewalk. I had goosebumps the morning we shot that ad — just thinking about all the years he’s spent loving and serving this town. In a minute, I’m going to tell you a bit about how Nettleton helped turn Brandon into the man he is today. [But first, can I respectfully ask you to chip in $25 or more today? Every donation makes a difference — we’re trying to raise $40,000 online before our public filing deadline on the 29th. This is our final deadline before the primary, so we really can’t miss this goal.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993850/418251500/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=) | **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993851/418251501/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)**
---
This little town of Nettleton instilled the deep values of public service that Brandon holds today. To give you an idea about how long he’s wanted to be involved in serving the people — when he was 10 years old, he came up with the idea of a Jr. Fire Department and tried his best to get the mayor and city leaders to listen to him! He wrote letters and even attended city council meetings!
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993852/418251502/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)
A letter that Brandon wrote when he was 10 years old
And Brandon got his first job right on Main Street in Nettleton. As a 13-year-old, Mr. Jimmy Rogers hired Brandon to work in his store, Rogers Auto Parts. He worked after school every day and all day on Saturdays and learned so much from Mr. Jimmy about the value of hard work, looking your best, keeping the store shelves stocked neatly and serving customers the right way.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993853/418251503/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)
Rogers Auto Parts store today (bonus photo of campaign pup, Harper!)
And, all of Mississippi needs to meet Miss Minnie Leeper of Nettleton. She was Brandon’s self-appointed campaign manager during his very first run for mayor of Nettleton way back in 2001 when he was 23 years old.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993854/418251504/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)
Miss Minnie stopping by the house recently to check on Brandon
Miss Minnie Leeper is a beloved fixture in our town — there’s no one in need she hasn’t tried to help. She calls Brandon regularly with advice or a request for him to help someone and then quickly hangs up on him before he can even get a word in!
This town raised Brandon and taught him the importance of giving back to your community. But too many small towns, just like Nettleton, have been left behind by Tate Reeves and his buddies. They don’t care about supporting small towns — they just care about where they’ll get their next big corporate check.
**Families in Mississippi need someone who knows where they’re from, has stood in their shoes, and who deeply loves our small communities across this state, like Nettleton. That’s why Brandon is running for governor — and I truly believe that he can make a difference for our state. In fact, I know he can!**
[I hope we can count on you to donate to Brandon’s campaign to be the next governor of Mississippi before the 29th. Any amount, no matter how small, helps us get the word out about Brandon’s positive vision for our state.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993855/418251505/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)
**[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66993856/418251506/960250762?amount=25&refcode=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&ms=ea.c74b47c6-7a2a-ee11-a9bb-00224832e1ba&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzc0YjQ3YzYtN2EyYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JdcOX33s7yhgx6_4PfNyAt5SGhTfBU1KRHGGWa2MTlI=)**
---
Thank you so much, and I hope to see you out on the campaign trail!

Greta Presley

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: We’re at a crossroads as an organization.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Over the last few months, we’ve sent emails being transparent about how
our fundraising has been in decline for more than a year. And we keep
sending these emails because the situation is dire.

We just had to lay off nine of our twenty members of staff.

Justice Democrats is at a crossroads as an organization. What we raise in
the coming weeks and months will set the trajectory of how we show up and
what we can accomplish this cycle and beyond.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/136120?refcode=em20230724-30050&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1001&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk ]Will you make your
first donation of $3 right now to ensure we
can sustain our organization and have the resources to continue to recruit
and elect change-making progressives like AOC, Cori Bush, and Jamaal
Bowman?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/136120?refcode=em20230724-30050&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk ]Donate $3

I want to be clear as Executive Director that I made the most responsible
decision that honors our work, the staff, and stakeholders like you. Our
work and the mission this organization set out with is so much bigger than
any one person, and our decision-making is rooted in what is best for our
strategy and mission.

I, and our entire team, don’t want to exist as an organization just to
exist.

There are plenty of progressive groups doing great work. But we want to
keep going because we believe our work is uniquely important in the
progressive ecosystem with our laser focus on primaries and holding
federal democratic leadership accountable when they don’t deliver.

Justice Democrats was started by just a handful of Bernie 2016 staffers
and super volunteers. We had just worked on this inspiring campaign for
president. Bernie put a progressive agenda front and center for millions
of people for the first time — myself included.

Money has always been one of the biggest things holding us back from doing
more. It’s the one thing that our establishment opponents have a seemingly
endless supply of and that we’re always working hard to raise just a
fraction of what they can raise.

Our work is only possible because of our grassroots supporters. I truly
mean that.

Thank you so much for being a part of our movement to challenge the entire
political establishment and elect more progressive changemakers.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/136120?refcode=em20230724-30050&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk ]→ Will you now dig deep and make your first contribution of
$3 right now to support our critical work?

Thank you.

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1023&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1024&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230724-30050-footer&t=1025&refcode2=30050_1203867__PxLDk&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1026&akid=30050%2E1203867%2E_PxLDk

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Dr. Shawn Wilson for Governor
subject: Baton Rouge: You’re invited
body:
image of ... [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/swlogo2.png]
Peter,
You’re invited to join us tomorrow, Tuesday, July 25th as we're hosting the Grand Opening of our Baton Rouge office . It’s a great opportunity to come and shake hands , visit with friends , and pick up some campaign paraphernalia .
This is our time to make sure Louisiana continues moving in the right direction .
Join us on July 25th from noon until 2 pm at 700 N. 10th St. in Baton Rouge .
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
Grand Opening of Shawn Wilson’s Baton Rouge Office
Tuesday, July 25, 2023
12-2 pm
700 North 10th Street
Baton Rouge, LA
To learn more about my candidacy visit wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com] .
With hope,
Shawn Wilson
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
ENDORSE [https://www.wilsonforla.com/endorse]

Baton Rouge, LA 70821
